field_description: Client-side provisional identifier used while waiting for the official `keyId` returned by the server. Production servers should preserve it and may apply basic length or character checks for security, but that validation is outside the mock-server scope.
Logical stream or grouping identifier (for example, a project or topic path).

Mandatory integrity field derived from `keyId`. Production servers should verify it using a signed or JWT-style check; the mock server may also use it as a test hook to force a configured non-ok status.

Server-derived schema for the value node, inferred from `keyId` with unique identifiers removed. A client may use a temporary local hint, but the authoritative kind comes from the server-derived interpretation of `keyId`.
Optional ISO language code for the value content. Different value nodes in the same document may use different languages.
Protocol-level string payload. Higher-level formats such as free text, numbers, booleans, dates, or encoded JSON are still serialized as strings.
Server-generated version used for optimistic sync checks so clients and servers can reject writes based on an older state.
UTC timestamp when the value node was created (server clock). Useful for ordering nodes by creation time, for example comments or chat messages.
UTC timestamp when the value node was last updated (server clock). Useful for support/debugging after version mismatches and for showing recent changes.
